problem: Why do perovskite solar cells degrade — what material instability causes it?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: The key to Why do perovskite solar cells degrade — what material instability causes it? is Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) — specifically Two-dimensional material stacking — which would help 3,000,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Accelerated degradation study with 7 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
